snd_fst_is_swap.solution:
Theorem snd_fst_is_swap : forall (p : natprod),
  (snd p, fst p) = swap_pair p.
Proof.
  intros p. destruct p as [n m]. simpl. reflexivity. Qed.
snd_fst_is_swap.explanation: Destruct the pair p into its components (n, m), then simpl reduces both sides to (m, n).

fst_swap_is_snd.solution:
Theorem fst_swap_is_snd : forall (p : natprod),
  fst (swap_pair p) = snd p.
Proof.
  intros p. destruct p as [n m]. simpl. reflexivity. Qed.
fst_swap_is_snd.explanation: Destruct the pair to expose its components, then both sides simplify to m.

list_funs.solution:
Fixpoint nonzeros (l:natlist) : natlist :=
  match l with
  | nil => nil
  | 0 :: t => nonzeros t
  | h :: t => h :: (nonzeros t)
  end.

Example test_nonzeros:
  nonzeros [0;1;0;2;3;0;0] = [1;2;3].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint oddmembers (l:natlist) : natlist :=
  match l with
  | nil => nil
  | h :: t => if odd h then h :: (oddmembers t) else oddmembers t
  end.

Example test_oddmembers:
  oddmembers [0;1;0;2;3;0;0] = [1;3].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Definition countoddmembers (l:natlist) : nat :=
  length (oddmembers l).

Example test_countoddmembers1:
  countoddmembers [1;0;3;1;4;5] = 4.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_countoddmembers2:
  countoddmembers [0;2;4] = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_countoddmembers3:
  countoddmembers nil = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
list_funs.explanation: nonzeros filters out zeros by pattern matching on 0. oddmembers uses the odd function to keep only odd elements. countoddmembers reuses oddmembers and length.

alternate.solution:
Fixpoint alternate (l1 l2 : natlist) : natlist :=
  match l1, l2 with
  | nil, _ => l2
  | _, nil => l1
  | h1 :: t1, h2 :: t2 => h1 :: h2 :: (alternate t1 t2)
  end.

Example test_alternate1:
  alternate [1;2;3] [4;5;6] = [1;4;2;5;3;6].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_alternate2:
  alternate [1] [4;5;6] = [1;4;5;6].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_alternate3:
  alternate [1;2;3] [4] = [1;4;2;3].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_alternate4:
  alternate [] [20;30] = [20;30].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
alternate.explanation: Pattern match on both lists simultaneously. If either is nil, return the other. Otherwise interleave by taking one element from each and recursing.

bag_functions.solution:
Fixpoint count (v : nat) (s : bag) : nat :=
  match s with
  | nil => 0
  | h :: t => if h =? v then S (count v t) else count v t
  end.

Example test_count1:              count 1 [1;2;3;1;4;1] = 3.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_count2:              count 6 [1;2;3;1;4;1] = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Definition sum : bag -> bag -> bag := app.

Example test_sum1:              count 1 (sum [1;2;3] [1;4;1]) = 3.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Definition add (v : nat) (s : bag) : bag := v :: s.

Example test_add1:                count 1 (add 1 [1;4;1]) = 3.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_add2:                count 5 (add 1 [1;4;1]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint member (v : nat) (s : bag) : bool :=
  match s with
  | nil => false
  | h :: t => if h =? v then true else member v t
  end.

Example test_member1:             member 1 [1;4;1] = true.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_member2:             member 2 [1;4;1] = false.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
bag_functions.explanation: count recurses through the bag comparing each element with eqb. sum is just app (concatenation). add prepends the value. member checks if any element matches.

bag_more_functions.solution:
Fixpoint remove_one (v : nat) (s : bag) : bag :=
  match s with
  | nil => nil
  | h :: t => if h =? v then t else h :: (remove_one v t)
  end.

Example test_remove_one1:
  count 5 (remove_one 5 [2;1;5;4;1]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_remove_one2:
  count 5 (remove_one 5 [2;1;4;1]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_remove_one3:
  count 4 (remove_one 5 [2;1;4;5;1;4]) = 2.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_remove_one4:
  count 5 (remove_one 5 [2;1;5;4;5;1;4]) = 1.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint remove_all (v:nat) (s:bag) : bag :=
  match s with
  | nil => nil
  | h :: t => if h =? v then remove_all v t else h :: (remove_all v t)
  end.

Example test_remove_all1:  count 5 (remove_all 5 [2;1;5;4;1]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_remove_all2:  count 5 (remove_all 5 [2;1;4;1]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_remove_all3:  count 4 (remove_all 5 [2;1;4;5;1;4]) = 2.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_remove_all4:  count 5 (remove_all 5 [2;1;5;4;5;1;4;5;1;4]) = 0.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint included (s1 : bag) (s2 : bag) : bool :=
  match s1 with
  | nil => true
  | h :: t => if member h s2 then included t (remove_one h s2) else false
  end.

Example test_included1:              included [1;2] [2;1;4;1] = true.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example test_included2:              included [1;2;2] [2;1;4;1] = false.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
bag_more_functions.explanation: remove_one removes the first occurrence of v. remove_all removes all occurrences. included checks if every element of s1 appears in s2, removing matched elements to handle multiplicity.

add_inc_count.solution:
Theorem add_inc_count : forall (v : nat) (s : bag),
  count v (add v s) = S (count v s).
Proof.
  intros v s. simpl. rewrite eqb_refl. reflexivity.
Qed.
add_inc_count.explanation: After simpl, the goal becomes: if v =? v then S (count v s) else count v s = S (count v s). Using eqb_refl (v =? v = true) completes the proof.

list_exercises.solution:
Theorem app_nil_r : forall l : natlist,
  l ++ [] = l.
Proof.
  intros l. induction l as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem rev_app_distr: forall l1 l2 : natlist,
  rev (l1 ++ l2) = rev l2 ++ rev l1.
Proof.
  intros l1 l2. induction l1 as [| h t IHt].
  - simpl. rewrite app_nil_r. reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite IHt. rewrite app_assoc. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem rev_involutive : forall l : natlist,
  rev (rev l) = l.
Proof.
  intros l. induction l as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite rev_app_distr. rewrite IHt. simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem app_assoc4 : forall l1 l2 l3 l4 : natlist,
  l1 ++ (l2 ++ (l3 ++ l4)) = ((l1 ++ l2) ++ l3) ++ l4.
Proof.
  intros l1 l2 l3 l4.
  rewrite app_assoc. rewrite app_assoc. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma nonzeros_app : forall l1 l2 : natlist,
  nonzeros (l1 ++ l2) = (nonzeros l1) ++ (nonzeros l2).
Proof.
  intros l1 l2. induction l1 as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - destruct h as [| h'].
    + simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
    + simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
Qed.
list_exercises.explanation: app_nil_r by induction on l. rev_app_distr by induction on l1 using app_nil_r and app_assoc. rev_involutive by induction using rev_app_distr. app_assoc4 by two applications of app_assoc. nonzeros_app by induction on l1 with case analysis on the head element.

eqblist.solution:
Fixpoint eqblist (l1 l2 : natlist) : bool :=
  match l1, l2 with
  | nil, nil => true
  | nil, _ => false
  | _, nil => false
  | h1 :: t1, h2 :: t2 => if h1 =? h2 then eqblist t1 t2 else false
  end.

Example test_eqblist1 :
  (eqblist nil nil = true).
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_eqblist2 :
  eqblist [1;2;3] [1;2;3] = true.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_eqblist3 :
  eqblist [1;2;3] [1;2;4] = false.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Theorem eqblist_refl : forall l:natlist,
  true = eqblist l l.
Proof.
  intros l. induction l as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite eqb_refl. rewrite <- IHt. reflexivity.
Qed.
eqblist.explanation: eqblist compares lists element-wise using eqb. eqblist_refl is proved by induction on l, using eqb_refl (n =? n = true) in the inductive step.

count_member_nonzero.solution:
Theorem count_member_nonzero : forall (s : bag),
  1 <=? (count 1 (1 :: s)) = true.
Proof.
  intros s. simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.
count_member_nonzero.explanation: After simpl, count 1 (1 :: s) reduces to S (count 1 s), and 1 <=? S (count 1 s) reduces to 0 <=? count 1 s which is always true.

remove_does_not_increase_count.solution:
Theorem remove_does_not_increase_count: forall (s : bag),
  (count 0 (remove_one 0 s)) <=? (count 0 s) = true.
Proof.
  intros s. induction s as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - destruct h as [| h'].
    + simpl. rewrite leb_n_Sn. reflexivity.
    + simpl. destruct (h' =? 0).
      * simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
      * simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
Qed.
remove_does_not_increase_count.explanation: Induction on s. Empty case is trivial. For cons, destruct the head: if 0, remove_one removes it so count decreases by 1 (use leb_n_Sn). If nonzero, the head is kept in both sides and we apply IH.

bag_count_sum.solution:
Theorem bag_count_sum : forall (v : nat) (s1 s2 : bag),
  count v (sum s1 s2) = count v s1 + count v s2.
Proof.
  intros v s1 s2. induction s1 as [| h t IHt].
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. destruct (h =? v).
    + simpl. rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
    + rewrite IHt. reflexivity.
Qed.
bag_count_sum.explanation: The count of v in the sum of two bags equals the sum of the counts. Proved by induction on s1, with case analysis on whether the head equals v.

involution_injective.solution:
Theorem involution_injective : forall (f : nat -> nat),
    (forall n : nat, n = f (f n)) -> (forall n1 n2 : nat, f n1 = f n2 -> n1 = n2).
Proof.
  intros f Hinv n1 n2 Hfeq.
  rewrite (Hinv n1). rewrite (Hinv n2).
  rewrite Hfeq. reflexivity.
Qed.
involution_injective.explanation: Using the involution hypothesis, rewrite n1 to f(f(n1)) and n2 to f(f(n2)). Since f(n1) = f(n2), we get f(f(n1)) = f(f(n2)).

rev_injective.solution:
Theorem rev_injective : forall (l1 l2 : natlist),
  rev l1 = rev l2 -> l1 = l2.
Proof.
  intros l1 l2 Hrev.
  rewrite <- (rev_involutive l1). rewrite <- (rev_involutive l2).
  rewrite Hrev. reflexivity.
Qed.
rev_injective.explanation: Since rev is an involution (rev_involutive), we rewrite l1 as rev(rev(l1)) and l2 as rev(rev(l2)). Then rev l1 = rev l2 gives us rev(rev(l1)) = rev(rev(l2)).

hd_error.solution:
Definition hd_error (l : natlist) : natoption :=
  match l with
  | nil => None
  | h :: _ => Some h
  end.

Example test_hd_error1 : hd_error [] = None.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_hd_error2 : hd_error [1] = Some 1.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example test_hd_error3 : hd_error [5;6] = Some 5.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
hd_error.explanation: Return None for empty list, Some h for non-empty list where h is the head.

option_elim_hd.solution:
Theorem option_elim_hd : forall (l:natlist) (default:nat),
  hd default l = option_elim default (hd_error l).
Proof.
  intros l default. destruct l as [| h t].
  - reflexivity.
  - reflexivity.
Qed.
option_elim_hd.explanation: Destruct l: for nil, both sides return default. For h :: t, both sides return h.

eqb_id_refl.solution:
Theorem eqb_id_refl : forall x, eqb_id x x = true.
Proof.
  intros x. destruct x as [n]. simpl. rewrite eqb_refl. reflexivity.
Qed.
eqb_id_refl.explanation: Destruct x to expose the nat inside Id, then simpl reduces eqb_id (Id n) (Id n) to n =? n, which equals true by eqb_refl.

update_eq.solution:
Theorem update_eq :
  forall (d : partial_map) (x : id) (v: nat),
    find x (update d x v) = Some v.
Proof.
  intros d x v. simpl. rewrite eqb_id_refl. reflexivity.
Qed.
update_eq.explanation: Unfold update and find. The lookup checks eqb_id x x which is true by eqb_id_refl, so it returns Some v.

update_neq.solution:
Theorem update_neq :
  forall (d : partial_map) (x y : id) (o: nat),
    eqb_id x y = false -> find x (update d y o) = find x d.
Proof.
  intros d x y o H. simpl. rewrite H. reflexivity.
Qed.
update_neq.explanation: Unfold update and find. The lookup checks eqb_id x y which is false by hypothesis H, so it falls through to find x d.
